_tokenSwap(uint256,uint256,address).details: Used for GNXToken to GNXToken swaps. Done through exchanging GNXTokens in an intial swap to GNXToken which is then swapped to another GNXToken.Should not be interacted with directly by user calls. This exists for other contracts to facilitate token swaps.
_tokenSwap(uint256,uint256,address).notice: helper function specifically for tokenSwap --> only meant for other exchange contracts ONLY FOR EXCHANGE CONTRACTS Helper function specifically for _tokenSwap / tokenSwap.Facilitates token to token swaps. Allows the circumvension of  double fees when changing from one GNXToken to another GNXToken.

params.minTokens: Minimum number of tokens expected from output.

addLiquidity(uint256,uint256).details: ERC20 LP tokens are minted equal to the GNXNative deposit made. Required deposit amount must be calculated in front-end. Equal deposit is enforced by contract to prevent price manipulation.
addLiquidity(uint256,uint256).notice: Ability to add liquidity to the pool (mints LP tokens) Add liquidity of GNXToken and GNXNative to this trading pool. Users are rewarded in LP which can be exchanged for liquidity and trading accrued fees.Liquidity of both tokens added must be equal. Technically only one param required but having two params for both tokens makes it clear that both are required to add liquidity.

returns._0: Quantity of LP tokens minted to msg.sender.

approve(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-approve}. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address.

buyNative(uint256,uint256).details: This function is akin to tokenSwawp. This is the base function for switching from GNXTokens to GNXNative.
buyNative(uint256,uint256).notice: Generic purchase function for GNXNative General purchase function where GNXTokens are sold in exchange  for GNXNative. Requires stating a minimum expected return to prevent price spoofing.

params.minNative: Minimum expected output quantity of GNXNative.

buyTokens(uint256,uint256).notice: Generic purchase function for GNXTokens. General purchase function for exchanging GNXNative for GNXTokens.

params.minTokens: Minimum number of tokens expected from output.

decimals().details: Returns the number of decimals used to get its user representation. For example, if `decimals` equals `2`, a balance of `505` tokens should be displayed to a user as `5.05` (`505 / 10 ** 2`). Tokens usually opt for a value of 18, imitating the relationship between Ether and Wei. This is the value {ERC20} uses, unless this function is overridden; NOTE: This information is only used for _display_ purposes: it in no way affects any of the arithmetic of the contract, including {IERC20-balanceOf} and {IERC20-transfer}.

decreaseAllowance(address,uint256).details: Atomically decreases the allowance granted to `spender` by the caller. This is an alternative to {approve} that can be used as a mitigation for problems described in {IERC20-approve}. Emits an {Approval} event indicating the updated allowance. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address. - `spender` must have allowance for the caller of at least `subtractedValue`.

increaseAllowance(address,uint256).details: Atomically increases the allowance granted to `spender` by the caller. This is an alternative to {approve} that can be used as a mitigation for problems described in {IERC20-approve}. Emits an {Approval} event indicating the updated allowance. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address.

name().details: Returns the name of the token.

nativeReserve().notice: View function for GNXNative reserve View function for GNXNative stored in this contract.

returns._0: Amount of GNXNative held in this contract.

nativeToToken(uint256).notice: Lookup function Lookup function to call current price for selling GNXNative to receive GNXToken.

params.nativeSold: Quantity of GNXNative to be sold.

removeLiquidity(uint256).notice: Exchange LPs for equal return of GNXToken and GNXNative. Burn LP tokens in exchange for GNXNative and GNXTokens based  on current reserves, and amount being submitted. Portion of accrued fees are also returned in the respective token. Potentially results in impermenant loss.

params.amount: Quantity of LP tokens submitted for exchange.

returns._0: Tuple of GNXNative output, and GNXToken output.

symbol().details: Returns the symbol of the token, usually a shorter version of the name.

tokenReserve().notice: View function for GNXToken reserve View function for GNXTokens stored in this contract

returns._0: Amount of GNXNative held in this contract

tokenToNative(uint256).notice: Lookup function Lookup function to call current price for selling GNXToken to receive GNXNative.

params.tokensSold: Quantity of GNXTokens to be sold.

transfer(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transfer}. Requirements: - `recipient` cannot be the zero address. - the caller must have a balance of at least `amount`.

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transferFrom}. Emits an {Approval} event indicating the updated allowance. This is not required by the EIP. See the note at the beginning of {ERC20}. Requirements: - `sender` and `recipient` cannot be the zero address. - `sender` must have a balance of at least `amount`. - the caller must have allowance for ``sender``'s tokens of at least `amount`.
